mission: Define the explicit regulatory, clinical-safety, privacy, accessibility, assurance, records, resilience, and change-control operating model that must run alongside Vecells implementation without flattening the corpus into a timeless checklist.

framework_catalog.source_version_context: The corpus treats DCB0129 as the governing manufacturer-side clinical safety frame and explicitly ties it to templates, agile implementation guidance, and incremental evidence updates.
framework_catalog.source_date_context: Phase 9 notes the DCB standards are under review in the March 2026-era corpus and should therefore stay version-aware rather than timeless.

framework_catalog.framework_name: DCB0160 deployment and use clinical risk management
framework_catalog.source_version_context: The corpus positions DCB0160 as the deployment-and-use counterpart to DCB0129, especially once live identity, telephony, assistive, and operational rollout are in scope.
framework_catalog.source_date_context: Phase 9 notes DCB0160 is under review in the same March 2026-era corpus context and therefore remains a versioned input.

framework_catalog.source_version_context: The corpus treats DTAC as spanning clinical safety, data protection, technical security, interoperability, and usability or accessibility rather than a single evidence upload.
framework_catalog.source_date_context: Phase 9 explicitly says DTAC guidance was refreshed in March 2026 and must be tracked through version metadata and standards watchlists.

framework_catalog.notes: The change-control model must route specific deltas into DTAC evidence refresh instead of assuming annual resubmission only.

framework_catalog.source_version_context: The corpus ties DSPT to continuous evidence production, incident reporting capability, and operational proof rather than a once-a-year spreadsheet exercise.
framework_catalog.source_date_context: Phase 9 explicitly references the 2024-25 CAF-aligned DSPT guidance and its stronger response-and-recovery posture.

framework_catalog.source_version_context: The corpus elevates retention, deletion, archive, and legal-hold control into first-class product objects rather than after-the-fact storage policy.
framework_catalog.source_date_context: Phase 9 states the HTML version is the most up-to-date source and should therefore be carried as source metadata, not flattened into an undated policy label.

framework_catalog.source_version_context: The corpus treats the 17-point NHS service standard, simple-health-content guidance, and joined-up channel experience as release-shaping controls.
framework_catalog.source_date_context: Phase 7 frames the service standard as part of the current NHS App standards pack rather than a later UX tidy-up.

framework_catalog.source_version_context: The corpus binds privacy to identity, contact provenance, telemetry minimization, embedded-channel restrictions, and explicit data-flow evidence rather than generic legal boilerplate.

framework_catalog.framework_name: IM1 pairing and material-change RFC routing
framework_catalog.source_version_context: The corpus treats IM1 pairing as a whole-product documentation review lane and states that material AI enhancement routes through formal RFC plus updated SCAL and related documents.
framework_catalog.source_date_context: Phase 8 describes current IM1 guidance for AI-containing products and explicitly ties it to material-change routing rather than initial timeless pairing only.

framework_catalog.source_version_context: The corpus treats the NHS App channel as one portal with embedded shell, manifest, jump-off, and demo/onboarding obligations rather than a separate product.
framework_catalog.source_date_context: Phase 7 preserves the current onboarding sequence of expression of interest, product assessment, Sandpit, AOS, limited release, and full release.

framework_catalog.source_version_context: The corpus treats SCAL as an evidence bundle tied to exact manifest, telemetry, safety, accessibility, and release tuples rather than a detached upload.
framework_catalog.source_date_context: Phase 7 routes SCAL after Sandpit, AOS, demo, and incident rehearsal in the current NHS App process.

framework_catalog.framework_name: NHS AI-enabled ambient scribing and documentation guidance
framework_catalog.source_version_context: The corpus uses current NHS guidance on ambient scribing and documentation support to require human oversight, training, audit, bias monitoring, and bounded intended use.
framework_catalog.source_date_context: Phase 8 explicitly cites current NHS guidance as the closest operating basis for assistive capabilities and uses it as a live change-control reference.

framework_catalog.source_version_context: The corpus distinguishes verified transcription from higher-function generative processing and uses that split to drive intended-use freeze and medical-purpose reassessment.
framework_catalog.source_date_context: Phase 8 frames this through current NHS guidance and MHRA-regulated device boundary language rather than fixed timeless classification.

framework_catalog.framework_name: Release/runtime/publication parity and standards watchlist governance
framework_catalog.source_version_context: The corpus treats runtime publication bundles, parity records, continuity evidence, release watch tuples, and standards dependency watchlists as first-class regulated-delivery controls.
framework_catalog.source_date_context: Phase 9 explicitly notes March 2026 DTAC refresh and the 2 March 2026 decommissioning of legacy developer and FHIR documentation endpoints, so standards and dependency hygiene remain time-sensitive.

source_file_refs: phase-2-identity-and-echoes.md#2G. Convergence into one request model and one workflow
phase-8-the-assistive-layer.md#8H. Release administration, regulatory routing, and rollback readiness
standards_version_context: Carry DCB0129 as the governing manufacturer-side safety basis with source-era review status preserved.
Treat safety evidence as incremental per rule, identity, telephony, booking, pharmacy, and assistive deltas.
workstreams.why_it_exists: Vecells changes patient safety posture whenever rule packs, identity binding, telephony evidence, booking confirmation, pharmacy outcomes, or assistive workflow effects change. The corpus requires those deltas to update the hazard log and safety case incrementally.
required_reviews_or_signoffs: Independent manufacturer Clinical Safety Officer signoff
blocking_release_conditions: Missing hazard-log or safety-case delta for a material clinical change
Unresolved safety preemption or urgent-diversion drift
Safety signoff attached to the wrong release candidate or stale evidence tuple
workstreams.cadence: Creation-time baseline, then per material safety-affecting change and before any release candidate that changes clinical behavior.
workstreams.notes: This closes the gap where safety evidence was treated as final-stage paperwork instead of phase-triggered engineering output.

workstreams.workstream_name: Deployment and use clinical risk workstream
source_file_refs: phase-2-identity-and-echoes.md#2H. Hardening, safety evidence, and the formal Phase 2 exit gate
phase-7-inside-the-nhs-app.md#7H. Sandpit, AOS, SCAL, and operational delivery pipeline
phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9G. Security operations, incident workflow, and just-culture reporting
standards_version_context: Carry DCB0160 as deployment/use safety authority rather than folding it into DCB0129.
Preserve CAF-aligned DSPT recovery expectations in the same operating model.
workstreams.why_it_exists: Live rollout, local session ownership, telephony capture, embedded-channel behavior, and assistive visibility all change the deployment-side risk picture. The corpus requires a separate deployment/use safety burden rather than only manufacturer documentation.
required_artifacts: Rollback and recovery clinical impact review
required_test_or_rehearsal_classes: incident and near-miss drill linkage to safety follow-up
blocking_release_conditions: Live rollout changes without deployment-side safety review
Material incident learnings not reflected in deployment safety posture
Rollback or recovery posture not clinically reviewed for changed flows
workstreams.cadence: Before new live rollout classes, before channel expansion, and after any incident or deployment posture change that alters clinical risk.
workstreams.notes: This keeps deployment/use safety independent from manufacturer signoff while still bound to the same release tuple.

source_file_refs: blueprint-init.md#10. Identity, consent, security, and policy
standards_version_context: Preserve GDPR and PECR as named channel and privacy obligations in the corpus instead of collapsing them into generic information governance.
DPIA reruns remain explicit triggered outputs for identity, embedded, assistive, and telemetry changes.
workstreams.why_it_exists: Identity evidence, contact provenance, notification delivery, embedded-channel handling, telemetry, and assistive subprocessors all change personal-data exposure. The corpus requires explicit privacy routing tied to engineering artifacts and release proof.
required_test_or_rehearsal_classes: wrong-patient hold and placeholder suppression tests
embedded artifact and outbound URL restriction tests
blocking_release_conditions: No current DPIA for a material identity, embedded, or assistive change
Telemetry or message delivery path leaks raw identifiers or PHI-bearing URLs
Patient-public, authenticated, and grant-scoped disclosure postures are not separated
workstreams.cadence: Creation-time baseline, then per disclosure-changing delta and before any release that changes audience, channel, telemetry, or subprocessor posture.
workstreams.notes: This closes the gap where partner onboarding evidence and privacy proof sat outside engineering artifacts.

standards_version_context: Preserve DTAC and DSPT technical-security expectations as live framework inputs with refreshed guidance context.
Treat trust zones, service identities, and PHI-safe telemetry as assurance evidence rather than infra folklore.
workstreams.why_it_exists: Identity/session controls, channel detection, secrets, webhook ingest, partner dependencies, and runtime trust boundaries all change the technical security burden. The corpus expects those deltas to surface as evidence and release blockers.
required_artifacts: Security test and incident readiness bundle
required_reviews_or_signoffs: Independent evidence freshness review for partner/vendor changes
required_test_or_rehearsal_classes: auth replay and session expiry suites
blocking_release_conditions: Trust-zone, gateway, or secrets changes without updated technical-security evidence
Supplier assurance freshness drift unresolved
Required assurance slices degraded or quarantined without bounded recovery posture
workstreams.cadence: Before release-candidate approval for security-affecting changes and continuously for vendor freshness and active trust slices.
workstreams.notes: Technical security stays tied to the same release tuple and watch posture as clinical and privacy evidence.

source_file_refs: phase-2-identity-and-echoes.md#Rule 5: keep IM1 out of the critical path for this phase
phase-7-inside-the-nhs-app.md#7A. Journey inventory, integration manifest, and onboarding pack
phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9H. Tenant configuration, standards watch, and long-term governance
standards_version_context: The corpus treats interoperability as proof-bearing across NHS login, IM1, booking, hub, messaging, pharmacy, and future partner channels.
Source-era onboarding packs and RFC routes remain attached to specific partner and release tuples.
workstreams.why_it_exists: Vecells must prove adapter contracts, authoritative proof-vs-transport semantics, supplier capability evidence, and partner onboarding readiness across external dependencies. The corpus does not allow partner evidence to sit outside engineering.
required_reviews_or_signoffs: Partner Onboarding Lead review where external approval is needed
blocking_release_conditions: Supplier or partner proof chain is weaker than the declared authoritative evidence model
Adapter contract changed without updated conformance packet
Partner onboarding evidence required by the corpus is missing or stale
workstreams.cadence: At integration creation-time, on material adapter or supplier changes, and before releases that widen external surface behavior.
workstreams.notes: This keeps proof-vs-transport discipline explicit across booking, messaging, hub, and pharmacy rails.

source_file_refs: phase-1-the-red-flag-gate.md#3B. The web experience, not a form dump
phase-7-inside-the-nhs-app.md#7G. Accessibility, service-standard, and mobile embedded UX hardening
blueprint-init.md#10. Identity, consent, security, and policy
standards_version_context: Preserve WCAG 2.2 AA as explicit source-era guidance, especially for target size and accessible authentication.
Keep NHS service-standard and content obligations tied to real route and shell coverage rather than generic intent.
workstreams.why_it_exists: The corpus repeatedly states that accessibility, content clarity, and service-standard compliance are architectural and assurance-bearing. Mobile embedded mode intensifies this, but the baseline web product already depends on it.
required_reviews_or_signoffs: Service Owner acceptance of route-level evidence
blocking_release_conditions: Route or shell change without updated accessibility coverage and content review
Visual-dominant surface lacks parity-safe fallback evidence
Embedded or public recovery copy fails same-shell accessible repair posture
workstreams.cadence: Per route-family or shell change, before release, and again for embedded-channel onboarding when Phase 7 becomes active.
workstreams.notes: This closes the gap where accessibility or content could be treated as cosmetic or post-build work.

workstreams.workstream_name: Records management and retention governance workstream
source_file_refs: blueprint-init.md#The same summary discipline now extends to records lifecycle and preservation
standards_version_context: Retain the Records Management Code as the active retention source, including the HTML-version-is-current note from the corpus.
Treat retention and deletion as governed lifecycle evidence, not storage leftovers.
workstreams.why_it_exists: Evidence artifacts, recordings, transcripts, deletion certificates, archive manifests, and legal holds all need governed retention classes and auditable disposition behavior. The corpus says this is part of system design.
required_artifacts: Retention schedule and artifact class map
required_reviews_or_signoffs: Data Protection Officer review for disposition changes
Governance approval for new artifact classes
blocking_release_conditions: New durable artifact without retention class and disposition path
Delete or archive behavior not bound to the current evidence graph
Legal-hold or preservation flow not auditable for affected artifact families
workstreams.cadence: Creation-time for new artifact families, continuously for lifecycle changes, and in periodic governance packs.
workstreams.notes: This closes the gap where retention and deletion could be treated as operational leftovers.

source_file_refs: phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9G. Security operations, incident workflow, and just-culture reporting
standards_version_context: The corpus uses CAF-aligned DSPT reporting and just-culture language as a live operating model for incidents and near misses.
Incident and reportability proof feeds assurance packs and deployment/use safety; it is not a side-channel.
workstreams.why_it_exists: The programme needs one governed workflow for incidents, reportability assessment, near misses, CAPA, and drills. The corpus explicitly rejects passive incident handling and hidden operational learning.
required_reviews_or_signoffs: Clinical or privacy review where the incident affects those domains
blocking_release_conditions: Active severe incident or unassessed reportable event on the same release or domain slice
Incident learnings not routed into safety, privacy, or resilience follow-up when required
No evidence-preservation path for the incident class under review
workstreams.cadence: Continuous operational use with periodic drill cadence and release blocking when unresolved incidents materially affect the changed area.
workstreams.notes: Near miss is a first-class governed object in this operating model, not informal team memory.

workstreams.workstream_name: NHS login, IM1, and partner onboarding evidence workstream
source_file_refs: phase-2-identity-and-echoes.md#Rule 5: keep IM1 out of the critical path for this phase
phase-2-identity-and-echoes.md#2H. Hardening, safety evidence, and the formal Phase 2 exit gate
standards_version_context: Preserve partner-onboarding obligations as source-era process work tied to exact redirect, scope, and product documentation sets.
Keep IM1 AI-change routing as material-change evidence, not as a generic future note.
workstreams.why_it_exists: The corpus says partner onboarding evidence is related to engineering artifacts, runtime proof, and safety documentation. Redirect URIs, scopes, pairing packs, and RFC bundles are therefore explicit workstream outputs.
required_reviews_or_signoffs: Security Lead review for redirect and secret posture
Clinical Safety Officer review where the partner pack names safety evidence
blocking_release_conditions: Partner-facing configuration changes without current onboarding pack or RFC as applicable
Live partner launch attempted on stale scope, redirect, or evidence inventory
Product documentation diverges from the exact release candidate or channel tuple
workstreams.cadence: Whenever partner configuration, product documentation burden, or material change routing changes; not every release, but always when the corpus says the partner pack must move.
workstreams.notes: This closes the gap where partner onboarding evidence was treated as outside engineering.

workstreams.workstream_name: Deferred NHS App SCAL and channel onboarding workstream
source_file_refs: phase-7-inside-the-nhs-app.md#7G. Accessibility, service-standard, and mobile embedded UX hardening
phase-7-inside-the-nhs-app.md#7H. Sandpit, AOS, SCAL, and operational delivery pipeline
standards_version_context: Preserve the current NHS App sequence of manifest, demo, Sandpit, AOS, SCAL, limited release, and full release as deferred channel work.
Do not relabel deferred Phase 7 obligations as baseline blockers unless that phase is explicitly opened.
workstreams.why_it_exists: The channel is real and its obligations are explicit in the corpus, but the current programme baseline defers this phase. The operating model therefore keeps the workstream visible, structured, and non-baseline until Phase 7 activates.
required_artifacts: Embedded accessibility and incident rehearsal evidence
required_reviews_or_signoffs: Clinical safety and privacy signoff before SCAL submission
blocking_release_conditions: Only when Phase 7 is active: no embedded release without current manifest parity, accessibility evidence, rehearsal proof, and SCAL bundle
Embedded route exposure or cohort widening drift from the approved release tuple
workstreams.cadence: Deferred until Phase 7, then creation-time onboarding plus pre-release and channel-widening evidence cycles.
workstreams.notes: This row exists to keep deferred obligations explicit without turning them into baseline blockers.

workstreams.workstream_name: Assistive and AI governance plus change-control workstream
source_file_refs: phase-8-the-assistive-layer.md#8A. Assistive capability contract, intended-use boundaries, and policy envelope
phase-8-the-assistive-layer.md#8H. Release administration, regulatory routing, and rollback readiness
phase-8-the-assistive-layer.md#8I. Pilot rollout, controlled slices, and formal exit gate
standards_version_context: Preserve source-era guidance that differentiates transcription from higher-function generative processing and ties material AI changes into DTAC, DCB, DPIA, SCAL, and RFC routing.
Assistive governance is conditional scope, but once active it has full release-blocking and signoff burden.
workstreams.why_it_exists: The corpus rejects the idea that assistive regulation is only model QA. Intended-use wording, medical-purpose boundary, visible workflow effect, supplier freshness, rollout slices, and rollback evidence all belong to one governed workstream.
required_artifacts: RFCBundle and SCAL delta where required
required_reviews_or_signoffs: Independent manufacturer Clinical Safety Officer signoff
Release Manager approval after independent approvals complete
blocking_release_conditions: Visible or widened assistive capability without frozen intended use and current regulatory routing assessment
Supplier assurance freshness drift unresolved
Required RFC, SCAL delta, DTAC delta, DPIA rerun, or safety-case delta not completed for the change class
workstreams.cadence: Optional until assistive rollout starts; then at creation-time, every material change, every visible rollout step, and continuously for supplier and trust freshness.
workstreams.notes: This closes the gap where intended-use change, medical-device boundary, and supplier assurance freshness could drift apart.

workstreams.workstream_name: Release, runtime, publication parity, and standards watchlist workstream
source_file_refs: phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9H. Tenant configuration, standards watch, and long-term governance
forensic-audit-findings.md#Finding 95 - The audit still omitted governance watch-tuple parity and recovery posture from release oversight
standards_version_context: Preserve refreshed standards metadata and legacy-doc decommissioning context as inputs to the watchlist, not background noise.
Carry runtime publication parity, continuity evidence, and release watch tuples as first-class assurance artifacts.
workstreams.why_it_exists: The corpus says route contracts, runtime publication bundles, parity records, trust slices, continuity evidence, and standards watchlists are all release controls. Governance, runtime, and operations must read the same tuple.
required_artifacts: Continuity evidence bundle for affected routes
required_reviews_or_signoffs: Independent domain approvers as routed by the changed surface
blocking_release_conditions: Approved release tuple and published runtime tuple do not match
Standards blockers or expired exceptions remain open
Affected routes lack current continuity or recovery disposition evidence
workstreams.cadence: Every release candidate and continuously while standards or dependency posture drifts.
workstreams.notes: This closes the gap where release/publication/runtime parity looked like DevOps housekeeping instead of regulated delivery proof.

workstreams.workstream_name: Operational resilience, restore, and recovery rehearsal workstream
standards_version_context: Preserve the CAF-aligned DSPT response-and-recovery emphasis and the corpus rule that restore proof matters more than backup configuration alone.
Recovery order of dependent systems remains explicit source metadata, not a hidden operational assumption.
workstreams.why_it_exists: The corpus treats restore, failover, chaos proof, runbook binding, and critical-function recovery as release and assurance evidence. Backups alone do not satisfy the operating model.
required_reviews_or_signoffs: Resilience and operations approval through Release Manager
Service Owner signoff on essential-function readiness
Independent review when recovery posture changes materially
blocking_release_conditions: Restore evidence stale for the current runtime tuple
Runbooks or recovery coverage not bound to the exact release watch tuple
Dependency recovery order changed without fresh rehearsal proof
workstreams.cadence: Pre-release for every material topology or dependency-order change, then continuously as rehearsal freshness decays.
workstreams.notes: This closes the gap where restore and recovery were treated as ops leftovers instead of governed evidence.

framework_control_mapping.control_summary: Every material change to red-flag rules, evidence assimilation, or urgent handling updates the hazard log, safety case, and sprint-time evidence incrementally.

change_control_trigger_matrix.blocking_condition: No release until wrong-patient, subject-switch, and disclosure-safe recovery behavior are re-proved for the changed identity/session tuple.

change_control_trigger_matrix.blocking_condition: No visible widen or resume until slice-contract evidence, trust freshness, and rollback proof are current.
source_refs: phase-8-the-assistive-layer.md#8I. Pilot rollout, controlled slices, and formal exit gate
change_control_trigger_matrix.notes: Mandatory assistive trigger class from the prompt.

change_control_trigger_matrix.change_class: Partner redirect, scope, sandbox, or approved-portal configuration change
change_control_trigger_matrix.blocking_condition: No live partner move until config, secrets, evidence pack, and runtime tuple all agree.
source_refs: phase-2-the-identity-and-echoes.md#2H. Hardening, safety evidence, and the formal Phase 2 exit gate
change_control_trigger_matrix.notes: Partner-specific but release-blocking when that configuration changes.

change_control_trigger_matrix.change_class: NHS App manifest, route exposure, Sandpit/AOS profile, or SCAL-related channel change
change_control_trigger_matrix.blocking_condition: Only if Phase 7 is active: no release until manifest, SCAL, accessibility, and environment-parity evidence are current.
source_refs: phase-7-inside-the-nhs-app.md#7H. Sandpit, AOS, SCAL, and operational delivery pipeline
change_control_trigger_matrix.notes: Deferred Phase 7 trigger class kept explicit, not silently relabelled as current baseline.

change_control_trigger_matrix.change_class: Standards refresh, watchlist finding, or dependency/documentation baseline change
change_control_trigger_matrix.blocking_condition: No release or widen action until refreshed standards, exceptions, and dependent recovery evidence are reconciled to the active tuple.
source_refs: phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#Phase 9 implementation rules
phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#Working scope
change_control_trigger_matrix.notes: Preserves March 2026-era standards refresh context instead of flattening it away.

change_control_trigger_matrix.change_class: Incident learning or near-miss-driven control update
change_control_trigger_matrix.blocking_condition: Affected release or live slice remains blocked until required CAPA or safety/privacy/resilience follow-up is complete.
source_refs: phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9G. Security operations, incident workflow, and just-culture reporting
change_control_trigger_matrix.notes: Operational learning explicitly feeds the regulated workstreams.

nodes.notes: Owns service consequence and phase signoff burden.
Independent safety approval for manufacturer-side changes.

nodes.role_name: Deployment and Use Clinical Safety Lead
nodes.notes: Independent deployment/use safety approval for live rollout changes.
Approves trust-zone, secrets, and supplier-security posture.
Approves route-level accessibility and content evidence.
Owns incident and near-miss workflow decisions.
Owns partner-specific onboarding evidence and RFC routing.
Owns intended-use freeze, assistive rollout, and model-governance review.
Final release approver after independent domain approvals complete.

edges.independence_rule: The release approver must not treat engineering authorship as a substitute for independent safety signoff.
edges.notes: Manufacturer clinical safety is an upstream gate for safety-affecting releases.

edges.independence_rule: Deployment/use safety approval must be distinct from runtime implementation ownership.
edges.notes: Live rollout and recovery posture must carry independent review.

edges.independence_rule: Privacy approval cannot be implied by security or product signoff alone.
edges.notes: Privacy and records reviews attach to candidate-bound artifacts.

edges.independence_rule: Security review must remain independent of the team authoring the runtime or dependency change.
edges.notes: Security and runtime tuple parity converge here.

edges.independence_rule: Adapter owners cannot self-approve changed proof semantics without interoperability review.
Accessibility/content evidence cannot be collapsed into visual QA or product intent alone.
approval_artifacts: SCAL bundle and NHS App channel evidence pack
edges.notes: Route and channel changes require independent experience assurance.

edges.independence_rule: Partner-pack completeness must be checked by the owner of the onboarding lane, not inferred from deployment readiness alone.
approval_artifacts: IM1 pairing and material-change RFC bundle
SCAL bundle and NHS App channel evidence pack
edges.notes: External onboarding evidence and internal release evidence are related but not interchangeable.

edges.independence_rule: Assistive rollout cannot self-approve intended-use changes, visible workflow effects, or supplier drift.
approval_artifacts: Assistive release candidate and regulatory routing pack
edges.notes: Assistive governance adds a distinct independent lane before release.

edges.independence_rule: Service accountability is required, but it does not replace independent domain approvals.
edges.notes: Service owner confirms consequence ownership after domain approvals exist.
